description: How money flows through a project — budgets, contracts, changes, costs, and payments
critical_rules: CE→CCO deduplication: NEVER sum change_events + commitment_change_orders for the same scope. Use GREATEST(CE total, CCO total).
Budget = PLANNED spend, not actual cost. Actual cost = committed + direct + approved changes.
Not all customers purchase financial tools. Exclude zero-financial-data projects from cost benchmarks.
CEs use snake_case change_reason (design_development). CCOs use title case (Design Development). Always use LOWER() for comparison.
Void CEs: filter with mapped_to_status != 'void'. Void CCOs: filter with status != 'Void'.

budget_cost_structure.label: Budget & Cost Structure
budget_cost_structure.description: The planned allocation of project funds

elements.description: Total approved budget for a project — the financial ceiling against which all costs are measured

databricks.aggregation: SUM(amount) GROUP BY project_id for total project budget; SUM(original_amount) for original budget

secondary_tables.note: projects.total_value is the contract-level value, not the detailed budget

databricks.ready_tables_note: budget_line_items is NOT ready in gold layer. Use projects.total_value as proxy.

gotchas: budget_line_items NOT READY in gold layer — cannot query directly yet
projects.total_value exists as a proxy but is contract value, not detailed budget
Not all customers purchase financial tools — absence means no tool, not no budget

elements.description: Individual row in the project budget — allocates dollars to a specific cost code/scope

databricks.sample_query: SELECT project_id, cost_code_id, original_amount, amount, (amount - original_amount) AS budget_growth FROM finance.budget_line_items WHERE company_id = ?

gotchas: budget_line_items NOT READY in gold layer
amount = PLANNED allocation, NOT actual cost
Variance = amount − (committed_cost + direct_cost + approved_changes)

elements.description: Classification code that categorizes costs by type of work — typically MasterFormat-based

databricks.sample_query: SELECT code, name, COUNT(DISTINCT company_id) as cos FROM finance.cost_codes GROUP BY code, name ORDER BY cos DESC LIMIT 50

gotchas: Cost code structures vary wildly between companies — some use MasterFormat 3-level, others use flat codes
First 2 digits of MasterFormat code = division = maps to building system
The Rosetta Stone between financial data and building elements

elements.description: Work Breakdown Structure code — adds a second dimension to cost classification (phase, location, category)

primary_columns.description: Primary key — used as FK on all financial line items

join_paths: All financial line items reference wbs_code_id (CE line items, CCO line items, DC line items, budget mods)
databricks.sample_query: SELECT w.wbs_code_flat_code, w.cost_code, w.cost_code_name, COUNT(*) FROM finance.wbs_codes w WHERE w.company_id = ? GROUP BY 1,2,3

gotchas: WBS adoption varies widely — some companies use 5+ segments, others none
wbs_code_id is THE key FK on all financial line items — this is the system mapping key
WBS 'Rework' or 'Warranty' segments are signals for rework cost detection

elements.description: Grouping code for reporting — distinct from cost codes, used for executive roll-ups

databricks.ready_tables_note: budget_codes NOT ready in gold layer

gotchas: budget_codes NOT READY in gold layer
Least standardized of the three code types — highly company-specific
Budget codes with 'Rework' or 'Warranty' are rework detection signals (339 cos validated)
Exclude environmental remediation codes from rework analysis

commitments.label: Commitments
commitments.description: Contracted obligations to pay for work

elements.description: Legally binding agreement between GC and subcontractor — largest category of project spend (70-85%)

primary_columns.description: Commitment title — often names the trade scope

databricks.filter: WHERE type = 'Subcontract' (or equivalent — check distinct values)
databricks.sample_query: SELECT title, status, vendor_id, COUNT(*) FROM finance.commitments WHERE company_id = ? AND type = 'Subcontract' GROUP BY 1,2,3

gotchas: Type field distinguishes subcontracts from POs — check DISTINCT values for exact strings
Vendor on commitment links Financial domain → Organizations domain
Title often contains trade info (e.g., 'Electrical Subcontract')

elements.description: Agreement to purchase materials, equipment, or services — typically for materials not in a subcontract

databricks.primary_columns: Same as Commitment (Subcontract) — distinguished by type field

gotchas: Same table as subcontracts — filtered by type
PO delivery tracking connects to Field Operations domain (daily_log_delivery)
Material PO delays are a primary schedule risk — connects to Material Shortage insight

elements.description: Cost-code-level detail within a commitment — budget-aligned allocation

primary_columns.description: Line item description — NLP signal for cost categorization

databricks.ready_tables_note: commitment_line_items NOT ready in gold layer

gotchas: commitment_line_items NOT READY in gold layer
Sum of line items by cost code = committed cost on corresponding budget line
Description is rich NLP signal for cost categorization

change_management.label: Change Management
change_management.description: How scope and cost changes flow through the system

elements.description: Documented scope/cost change — the initial identification before formal commitment modification

primary_columns.description: FK to originating record — 91.5% NULL, bigint NOT a category

databricks.ready_tables_note: change_events NOT ready in gold layer. Accessible via direct Databricks SQL.

gotchas: change_event_id NOT 'id' — validated column name correction
change_reason is the PRIMARY categorization — NOT origin_id (bigint FK, 91.5% null)
origin_type values: 'Rfi::Header' (307K), 'Observations::Item', 'Meeting'
mapped_to_status != 'void' to exclude voided CEs
change_reason uses snake_case: design_development (NOT title case like CCOs)
DEDUP RULE: CEs and CCOs track the SAME change at different lifecycle stages — NEVER sum them

elements.description: Cost-code-level detail within a CE — breaks estimated change cost into budget-aligned allocations

primary_columns.description: FK to finance.wbs_codes — maps to cost code → building system
Line item description — rich NLP signal
Link to CPCO line item — 40.8% populated

gotchas: latest_cost_amount is the correct cost column (NOT 'amount')
wbs_code_id maps to cost code → MasterFormat → building system
commitment_potential_change_order_line_item_id: 40.8% populated — most reliable CE→CCO tracing path
CPCO line items table NOT in gold layer — tracing path limited

elements.description: Intermediate instrument bridging CE to CCO — GC's request to modify a specific subcontract

databricks.ready_tables_note: Table exists but NOT ready in gold layer

gotchas: CPCO is the bridge: CE identifies → CPCO formalizes → CCO executes
commitment_change_order_id is 89.5% populated — good CCO linkage
CPCO LINE ITEMS table NOT in gold layer — limits line-item-level tracing

elements.description: Formal, approved modification to a subcontract/PO — changes committed dollar amount

validated_stats.avg_cco_vs_ce: CCOs are 6.4x larger than CEs on average

gotchas: change_reason is TITLE CASE ('Design Development'), NOT snake_case like CEs — always use LOWER() for comparison
grand_total is the cost column — NOT 'amount'
status: 'Approved' (96.7%), 'Pending', 'Draft', 'Void', 'Proceeding'
CCOs have their OWN change_reason — independent of CE. CCOs can exist WITHOUT a corresponding CE.
No direct FK to change_events in gold layer — use commitment_potential_change_orders as bridge
commitment_change_order_line_items has NO change_event_id or change_event_line_item_id
$1.45B saved from CE/CCO deduplication in rework analysis

elements.description: Cost-code-level detail within a CCO — modifies specific commitment line item amounts

primary_columns.description: FK to finance.wbs_codes — EXISTS directly (Q15 was wrong, Q20c confirmed)

gotchas: wbs_code_id EXISTS directly on this table (Q15 was wrong — confirmed in Q20c)
extended_amount is the cost column
commitment_potential_change_order_id is 100% populated — reliable CPCO link
NO change_event_id or change_event_line_item_id — no direct FK to CEs

elements.description: Formal modification to the prime contract between owner and GC — changes contract value

databricks.ready_tables_note: prime_contract_change_orders NOT ready in gold layer

gotchas: NOT ready in gold layer
Distinct from commitment COs — owner COs change GC revenue, not sub cost
Delta between owner CO and commitment CO amounts = GC margin on changes
Lower adoption than commitment-side change management

elements.description: Internal budget transfer between line items — moves money without changing total budget

primary_columns.description: Direct FK to finance.wbs_codes — no hop needed

databricks.ready_tables_note: budget_modifications NOT ready in gold layer

gotchas: transfer_amount (NOT 'amount') — validated column name correction
notes (NOT 'description') — validated column name correction
wbs_code_id exists directly — no need for budget_line_items hop
Modifications TO rework-coded lines = actual rework exceeded planned budget
The transfer_amount is a budget adjustment, NOT a standalone cost transaction

cost_tracking.label: Cost Tracking
cost_tracking.description: Non-commitment expenditures — costs that bypass subcontract/PO workflow

elements.description: Non-commitment expenditure — GC self-performed labor, small purchases, equipment rental, T&M, reimbursables

databricks.sample_query: SELECT description, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM finance.direct_costs WHERE company_id = ? GROUP BY description ORDER BY cnt DESC

gotchas: Direct costs bypass commitment/invoice workflow — charge against budget directly
Companies that self-perform more have higher direct cost ratios
Represents 15-30% of project cost depending on self-perform scope

elements.description: Cost-code-level detail within a direct cost — enables granular non-commitment cost tracking

primary_columns.description: Line item description — NLP signal for rework keywords

databricks.sample_query: SELECT dl.description, dl.amount, w.cost_code, w.cost_code_name FROM finance.direct_cost_line_items dl JOIN finance.wbs_codes w ON dl.wbs_code_id = w.wbs_code_id WHERE dl.company_id = ? LIMIT 100

gotchas: Description contains actionable keywords: 'rework', 'repair', 'correction', 'redo'
$500M in rework-related direct costs identified via NLP on descriptions
wbs_code_id maps to cost code → building system for system-level cost attribution

elements.description: Periodic billing from GC to owner — request for payment under the prime contract

secondary_tables.note: Approval workflow — timestamps for processing time calculation

databricks.sample_query: SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM finance.owner_invoices WHERE company_id = ? GROUP BY status

gotchas: Pay app amount ≠ project cost — it's billing, not cost
Monthly billing cycle drives the financial rhythm of every project
Workflow tables enable invoice processing time calculation

elements.description: Periodic billing from subcontractor to GC for work completed under a subcontract

databricks.sample_query: SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM finance.subcontractor_invoices WHERE company_id = ? GROUP BY status

gotchas: Mirrors pay applications but flows opposite direction — subs bill the GC
Invoice processing time directly affects sub cash flow
Sub invoice line items NOT ready in gold layer — header only

elements.description: Funds withheld from progress payments as security — released at completion

databricks.note: No dedicated retainage table — tracked within invoicing system

gotchas: Retainage is NOT a cost — it's a withheld portion of earned payment
Typical rate: 5-10%. Release triggers: substantial completion, final completion
Some states cap retainage at 5% — jurisdiction-specific

elements.description: Actual transfer of funds — the final step in the billing cycle

primary_tables.description: Final payments issued by the company

gotchas: Payment is the actual cash event — distinct from invoice approval
Pay-when-paid clauses mean sub payment depends on owner payment to GC
Multiple tables cover different aspects of the payment lifecycle

elements.description: Legal document waiving right to file mechanic's lien — exchanged with each payment
gotchas: Four types: conditional/unconditional × progress/final
Missing lien waivers can block final payment and retainage release
Some states have statutory forms that must be used exactly

financial_classification.label: Financial Classification & Structure
financial_classification.description: How costs are organized and categorized

elements.description: Cost breakdown defining billing structure — line items represent discrete billable portions of work

databricks.note: No dedicated SOV table — embedded in commitment and billing structure

gotchas: SOV is a BILLING structure, not a cost structure — may not align with cost codes
Front-loading (billing more early) distorts true percent complete
SOV lines often describe building elements in plain language — NLP opportunity

elements.description: Budget item for undefined-scope work — price estimated and reconciled to actual

databricks.note: No dedicated allowance tracking — managed via budget codes and manual processes

gotchas: No dedicated Procore feature for allowance tracking
Allowances are NOT contingency — they are placeholders for defined scope
Exceeded allowances become change orders

elements.description: Budget reserve for unforeseen costs — not allocated to specific scope until needed

databricks.note: No dedicated contingency tracking — managed via budget line items and budget codes

gotchas: Contingency is NOT a cost — it's a RESERVE
Burn rate > % complete = red flag for project financial health
Requires manual identification of contingency budget lines

earned_value_forecasting.label: Earned Value & Forecasting
earned_value_forecasting.description: Derived financial measures computed from transactional instruments

elements.description: Projection of total project cost at completion — actual cost to date + estimated cost to complete

databricks.ready_tables_note: budget_forecast_line_items NOT ready in gold layer. Sparse adoption.

gotchas: EAC is computed, not stored as standalone — included because it's the most important derived measure
Most EAC work done in Excel, not Procore
Sparse adoption limits benchmarking value

elements.description: Time-phased projection of money in (owner payments) and money out (sub payments, direct costs)

databricks.note: Not a native Procore feature — most GCs manage in Excel or ERP

gotchas: Not a native Procore feature — lowest coverage element in Financial Instruments
Can be computed from payment and invoice data
S-curve distribution typical — cash flow connects to Schedule domain

standard_measures.label: Standard Measures
standard_measures.description: KPIs and metrics derived from financial instrument performance

elements.description: Budget Amount − (Committed Cost + Direct Cost + Approved Changes)

databricks.grain: Per cost code, per building system, or project-level

elements.description: (Final Cost − Original Budget) / Original Budget × 100
Earned Value / Actual Cost — measures cost efficiency

databricks.note: True EVMS rare in commercial construction — simplified CPI more practical

elements.description: Total Committed / Budget × 100 — tracks buy-out progress
(Total CO Value / Original Contract Value) × 100

databricks.note: Separate by change_reason for root cause decomposition

elements.description: (Contingency Used / Original Contingency) / (% Complete)

databricks.note: One of the few LEADING financial indicators

elements.description: Approval Date − Submission Date (in business days)

databricks.note: Prompt Payment Acts require payment within 30 days in many states

elements.description: Actual Cash Position − Projected Cash Position

databricks.note: Requires cash flow projection capability — most users manage in Excel/ERP

elements.description: EAC − Original Budget — tracks how projected final cost evolves

databricks.note: EAC variance at 25% complete is a strong predictor of final cost growth

join_reference.description: Key join paths across financial tables

paths.path: line_item.wbs_code_id → wbs_codes.cost_code_id → cost_codes.code (first 2 digits = MasterFormat division → building system)

change_events → change_event_line_items.commitment_potential_change_order_line_item_id → (CPCO line items NOT in gold) OR commitment_potential_change_orders.commitment_change_order_id → commitment_change_orders
paths.confidence: Medium — CPCO line items table missing from gold layer

gold_layer_readiness.note: NOT READY means not flagged as importable in gold layer catalog, but many are still queryable via direct Databricks SQL on the finance schema.
